rag_rules: Fix unresolved port references in AGREE contracts by ensuring target ports are valid and reachable within the component type scope.
Ensure AGREE guarantees referencing ports are bound to visible AADL features or declared helpers.
generic_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid type errors.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are resolvable within the current scope.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must end with a valid closing brace and semicolon; missing EOF errors indicate syntax issues in expressions.
Mode transition guarantees should explicitly define the mode variable and its valid range.
anti_patterns: Do not reference ports or features that are not visible or resolvable in the current AADL scope.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or inadequate port kinds
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing EOF in expressions
